2021-12-01.title: Omicron Variant, in at Least 20 Nations, Spread Earlier Than Was Known
2021-12-01.abstract: With evidence growing that a worrisome new coronavirus variant is highly contagious, health officials issued warnings that vulnerable people should not travel.

2021-12-01.title: LeBron James enters N.B.A. health and safety protocols.
2021-12-01.abstract: Comments from Anthony Davis indicated James has tested positive for the coronavirus, which could keep him out for 10 days.

2021-12-01.title: E.U. Interpreter Says Greece Expelled Him to Turkey in Migrant Roundup
2021-12-01.abstract: The man’s story echoes complaints from human-rights groups that Greek authorities often expel asylum seekers indiscriminately and violently.

2021-12-01.title: The Next Challenge to Vaccinating Africa: Overcoming Skepticism
2021-12-01.abstract: Vaccines are finally available in many African countries, but an underfunded public health system has slowed their delivery, and some people there, as well as in South Asia, are wary of taking them.

2021-12-01.title: A Game Designer in Beijing Bought Toy Guns. China Imprisoned Him.
2021-12-01.abstract: San Cheng bought toy guns online as props for video game design. Then the police arrived, and he was jailed under China’s strict but messy gun laws.

2021-12-02.title: Citing Covid-19, Climate and Wars, U.N. Asks Donors for Big Jump in Funding
2021-12-02.abstract: The world body said it needed $41 billion to fund humanitarian aid to help 183 million people in 63 countries.

2021-12-02.title: ‘Call Me Dog Tag Man’: Pacific Island Is Full of War Relics and Human Remains
2021-12-02.abstract: More than 75 years after the Battle of Biak ended, collectors are still finding remnants of the fight, and U.S. authorities are hoping to bring closure to families of soldiers still missing.

2021-12-02.title: Millions More People Got Access to Water. Can They Drink It?
2021-12-02.abstract: The U.N. pledged to halve the proportion of the world without access to clean drinking water by 2015.

2021-12-02.title: Extreme Poverty Has Been Sharply Cut. What Has Changed?
2021-12-02.abstract: The U.N. pledged to cut by half the proportion of people living in the worst conditions around the world.

2021-12-02.title: Migration Talking Points Surge in France, but Not Migration
2021-12-02.abstract: With a presidential election looming, French presidential hopefuls are hardening their positions against immigration even as other countries compete for migrant workers.

2021-12-03.title: Prior Infection Is Little Defense Against Virus Variant, Scientists Say
2021-12-03.abstract: Evidence from South Africa, where the Omicron variant already dominates, shows a high rate of reinfection of people who have already had the coronavirus.

2021-12-03.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in December
2021-12-03.abstract: Our picks for December, Including ‘The Power of the Dog,’ ‘Don’t Look Up’ and ‘The Lost Daughter’

2021-12-03.title: A Slow-Motion Climate Disaster: The Spread of Barren Land
2021-12-03.abstract: Brazil’s northeast, long a victim of droughts, is now effectively turning into a desert. The cause? Climate change and the landowners who are most affected.

2021-12-03.title: Philippine Court Allows Maria Ressa to Attend Nobel Peace Prize Ceremony
2021-12-03.abstract: The decision came after days of growing international pressure on the government to allow the journalist to attend the ceremony in Norway.

2021-12-03.title: How Long Can Students Wait for Australia to Reopen?
2021-12-03.abstract: As the Omicron variant heralds more uncertainty, international students weigh up their options.

2021-12-04.title: Short-handed hospitals in upstate New York fill up as Covid cases surge.
2021-12-04.abstract: A surge in Covid cases and a shortage of health care workers are filling hospitals and nursing homes past their capacity in upstate New York, creating a growing crisis in the health care system even before the Omicron variant is known to have spread through the area, according to hospital executives from Buffalo to Albany.

2021-12-04.title: Congo Ousts Mining Leader in a Cloud of Corruption Claims
2021-12-04.abstract: The country’s president removed Albert Yuma Mulimbi as chairman of the state mining firm. Cobalt in Congo is a crucial resource in the global clean energy revolution.

2021-12-04.title: F.D.A. authorizes Eli Lilly antibody treatment for high-risk young children.
2021-12-04.abstract: Doctors have a new option for the youngest patients who are vulnerable to the worst outcomes from Covid.

2021-12-04.title: Belgian Port City Grapples With a Flood of Cocaine
2021-12-04.abstract: Antwerp has become the main port of entry into Europe for the drug, which is being blamed for a surge of violence that has prompted some Belgian officials to call for a war on drugs.

2021-12-04.title: In France, a Right-Wing Polemicist Tries Channeling De Gaulle to Win Votes
2021-12-04.abstract: Éric Zemmour has adopted imagery reminiscent of Charles de Gaulle, the wartime leader. But his call for reborn glory for France is sharply at odds with the realities of the country today.

2021-12-05.title: ‘It felt like a wedding,’ says a refugee Francis brought to Rome from Lesbos in 2016.
2021-12-05.abstract: Three Syrian families who traveled on the papal plane after the pope’s last visit to the Greek island have made new lives for themselves in Italy’s capital.

2021-12-05.title: Fatalities Reported After Military Truck Rams Protesters in Myanmar
2021-12-05.abstract: Witnesses said soldiers also fired into the crowd and kicked wounded demonstrators, the latest in a series of confrontations in which the military’s behavior has infuriated citizens.

2021-12-05.title: On Syria’s Ruins, a Drug Empire Flourishes
2021-12-05.abstract: Powerful associates of Syria’s president, Bashar al-Assad, are making and selling captagon, an illegal amphetamine, creating a new narcostate on the Mediterranean.

2021-12-05.title: When the Biggest Spenders Aren’t Coming Back Any Time Soon
2021-12-05.abstract: Even before Omicron’s arrival, China was discouraging its citizens from traveling abroad. That has had a huge impact on global tourism.

2021-12-05.title: Anger Spreads in Northeastern India After Security Forces Kill 14 Civilians
2021-12-05.abstract: Eight mine workers were shot in a mistaken ambush by soldiers seeking insurgents, and six protesters died later in clashes with government forces, stoking fears of further violence in the restive region.

2021-12-06.title: Europe is on edge as Omicron cases rise quickly before the holidays.
2021-12-06.abstract: LONDON — Confirmed cases of the Omicron variant surged in Britain and Denmark on Sunday, backing up scientists’ fears that it has already spread more widely despite travel bans and adding to worries of new lockdowns before the holidays.

2021-12-06.title: Norodom Ranariddh, Royal Player in Cambodian Politics, Dies at 77
2021-12-06.abstract: He rode a wave of royalist sentiment to win a United Nations-sponsored election in 1993, but was later ousted in a power struggle with his co-prime minister.

2021-12-06.title: Companies Linked to Russian Ransomware Hide in Plain Sight
2021-12-06.abstract: Cybersecurity experts tracing money paid by American businesses to Russian ransomware gangs found it led to one of Moscow’s most prestigious addresses.

2021-12-06.title: New variant means China’s big spenders won’t be traveling any time soon.
2021-12-06.abstract: This was supposed to be the year travel came back. In Europe and Asia, many countries reopened their airports and welcomed tourists. But they are confronting a new reality: Variants such as Omicron are causing global panic, leading governments to shut borders again, and their biggest spenders — Chinese tourists — aren’t returning any time soon.

2021-12-06.title: Aung San Suu Kyi Falls, but Myanmar’s Democratic Hopes Move On
2021-12-06.abstract: The ousted civilian leader faces years in custody after being sentenced on the first of several charges. In her absence, a new generation of younger, more progressive politicians is emerging.

2021-12-07.title: Can Germany’s New Chancellor Revive the Left in Europe?
2021-12-07.abstract: Olaf Scholz wants to win back workers who defected to the populist far right. Success could make him a model for Social Democrats everywhere.

2021-12-07.title: Ahead of Biden’s Democracy Summit, China Says: We’re Also a Democracy
2021-12-07.abstract: Beijing argues that its system represents a distinctive form of democracy, one that has dealt better than the West with challenges like the pandemic.

2021-12-07.title: Who Are Congo’s Cobalt Entrepreneurs?
2021-12-07.abstract: The Democratic Republic of Congo’s vast supply of cobalt is attracting an international cast of investors, including an R&B singer and a basketball star.

2021-12-07.title: Sri Lanka’s Plunge Into Organic Farming Brings Disaster
2021-12-07.abstract: The economically troubled country banned chemical fertilizers without preparing farmers, prompting a surge in food prices and worries about shortages.

2021-12-07.title: On the Banks of the Furious Congo River, a 5-Star Emporium of Ambition
2021-12-07.abstract: As the clean energy revolution upends the centuries-long lock of fossil fuels on the global economy, dealmakers and hustlers converge on the Fleuve Congo Hotel.

2021-12-08.title: The new government promises to be tougher on China and Russia.
2021-12-08.abstract: Though its plans are aspirational for the moment, its commitments to Europe, NATO and the trans-Atlantic relationship are solid.

2021-12-08.title: Beijing Silenced Peng Shuai in 20 Minutes, Then Spent Weeks on Damage Control
2021-12-08.abstract: Chinese propaganda officials have tried to shape the global discussion of the tennis player Peng Shuai’s #MeToo accusations, but their top-down strategy has largely stumbled.

2021-12-08.title: Just in Time for Christmas: Knitwear Fit for a T. Rex
2021-12-08.abstract: The Natural History Museum in London outfitted its animatronic Tyrannosaurus rex in a colorful Christmas sweater.

2021-12-08.title: India’s Top Military General Dies in Helicopter Crash
2021-12-08.abstract: Gen. Bipin Rawat, who was killed along with his wife and 11 others on board, was in charge of overhauling a military that has struggled to modernize.

2021-12-08.title: Amid new U.K. restrictions, the government is accused of flouting rules last Christmas.
2021-12-08.abstract: Critics have condemned a recording showing the prime minister’s staff joking about breaches of coronavirus restrictions last year, when Britain was in lockdown.

2021-12-09.title: Jailed Journalists Reach Record High for Sixth Year in 2021
2021-12-09.abstract: The Committee to Protect Journalists, a press freedom monitoring group, said 293 journalists were behind bars this year, more than a quarter of them in China.

2021-12-09.title: Indonesian Militant Sentenced to Life in Prison Over 2005 Bombing
2021-12-09.abstract: A court said Upik Lawanga made bombs for an attack that killed 22 people at a market. He is a member of a terrorist group that bombed churches, hotels and a Bali nightclub in the 2000s.

2021-12-09.title: Ukraine Commanders Say a Russian Invasion Would Overwhelm Them
2021-12-09.abstract: If Russia opts for an invasion, Ukraine’s generals say, they would have no hope of repelling it without a major infusion of military help from the West.

2021-12-09.title: Progress for Saudi Women Is Uneven, Despite Cultural Changes and More Jobs
2021-12-09.abstract: Women say Saudi Arabia has advanced significantly in just the past year, with more choices regarding work, fashion (including colored abayas) and social spaces, but restrictions remain everywhere.

2021-12-09.title: New Zealand Plans to Eventually Ban All Cigarette Sales
2021-12-09.abstract: The proposal, expected to become law next year, would raise the smoking age year by year until it covers the entire population.

2021-12-10.title: Mexico Migrant Truck Crash Leaves at Least 54 Dead
2021-12-10.abstract: Over 100 others were reported injured in the crash in southern Chiapas State, where many migrants regularly cross into Mexico from Central America.

2021-12-10.title: Rain-Soaked Rural Australia Asks: When Will It End?
2021-12-10.abstract: After years of climate extremes, even the toughest, most community-minded Australians are desperate for a break from the deluge of disaster.

2021-12-10.title: Taiwan Loses Nicaragua as Ally as Tensions With China Rise
2021-12-10.abstract: The nation switched diplomatic allegiance to Beijing, leaving 13 nations and the Vatican still recognizing Taiwan as a sovereign nation.

2021-12-10.title: Earth Is Getting a ‘Black Box’ to Hold Humans Accountable for Climate Change
2021-12-10.abstract: When a plane crashes, its flight recorder is critical to piecing together the missteps that led to calamity. Now the planet is getting its own in case it self-destructs.

2021-12-10.title: Turkey’s President Follows His Own Advice Even as Economy Slips
2021-12-10.abstract: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan is increasingly isolated as he clings to an economic prescription that few endorse but that he believes will bear results before elections.

2021-12-11.title: British studies warn of Omicron’s speed, and one notes the need for boosters.
2021-12-11.abstract: The first real-world study of how vaccines hold up against the Omicron variant showed a significant drop in protection against symptomatic cases caused by the new and fast-spreading form of the coronavirus.

2021-12-11.title: California official says she was stalked by members of an anti-vaccine group.
2021-12-11.abstract: The president of California’s medical board was “followed and confronted” in a dark parking garage in the city of Walnut Creek by a group that promotes misinformation about the coronavirus pandemic, she wrote on social media this week.

2021-12-11.title: First Fires, Then Floods: Climate Extremes Batter Australia
2021-12-11.abstract: Many of the same areas that suffered through horrific bush fires in 2019 and 2020 are now dealing with prodigious rainfall that could leave some people stranded for weeks.

2021-12-11.title: Silent Films Offer Rare Glimpses of Life in 1920s Ireland
2021-12-11.abstract: Shot by an American ornithologist in the early years of Irish independence, the footage turned up in the archives of the Chicago Academy of Sciences and is being restored.

2021-12-11.title: Search for Indigenous Children Takes New Step, Calling Police
2021-12-11.abstract: The Six Nations in Ontario have set up a special group to oversee the investigation of student deaths, in a rare collaboration between an Indigenous group and the police.

2021-12-12.title: ‘Our Boat Was Surrounded by Dead Bodies’: Witnessing a Migrant Tragedy
2021-12-12.abstract: Migrants who were on a separate boat described the horrible aftermath of the sinking in the English Channel that took at least 27 lives.

2021-12-12.title: Haiti’s Leader Kept a List of Drug Traffickers. His Assassins Came for It.
2021-12-12.abstract: In the months before his murder, President Jovenel Moïse took a number of steps to fight drug and arms smugglers. Some officials now fear he was killed for it.

2021-12-12.abstract: Prime Minister Naftali Bennett traveled to Abu Dhabi on Sunday, the first such visit by an Israeli leader, with plans to meet with the Emirati prince.

2021-12-12.title: New Caledonia Says ‘Non’ to Independence
2021-12-12.abstract: The vote on the Pacific island territory comes as France’s president has prioritized shoring up the country’s international profile, seeing its military  as a bulwark against China.

2021-12-12.title: Diplomats Warn Russia of ‘Massive Consequences’ if It Invades Ukraine
2021-12-12.abstract: The foreign ministers for the Group of 7 urged Russia to pull back from the tense border standoff. Moscow has massed as many as 100,000 troops on Ukraine’s eastern, northern and southern frontiers.

2021-12-13.title: Hong Kong Court Sentences Jimmy Lai to Prison Over Tiananmen Vigil
2021-12-13.abstract: The former media mogul and other prominent pro-democracy activists were previously convicted of inciting others to take part in an unauthorized assembly.

2021-12-13.title: Across the World, Covid Anxiety and Depression Take Hold
2021-12-13.abstract: It is still unclear how much of a threat the fast-spreading Omicron variant poses, but fear and a sudden revival of restrictions have added to an epidemic of loneliness.

2021-12-13.title: South Korea Has Long Wanted Nuclear Subs. A New Reactor Could Open a Door.
2021-12-13.abstract: The country plans to build a small modular reactor for marine propulsion, raising questions of whether it eventually intends to develop a nuclear submarine despite a U.S. treaty.

2021-12-13.title: India’s Latest Religious and Cultural Flashpoint: Eggs
2021-12-13.abstract: Food-cart rules spurred by conservative beliefs draw a backlash, showcasing the tensions around the country’s rising Hindu nationalist movement.

2021-12-13.abstract: Cementing an alliance built on mutual fear of Iran, Prime Minister Naftali Bennett met Prince Mohammed bin Zayed of the United Arab Emirates on the first official visit by an Israeli leader to the Gulf state.

2021-12-14.title: Tunisia’s President Promises Vote on Constitution and Sets Election Date
2021-12-14.abstract: President Kais Saied’s announcement dispelled some of the uncertainty Tunisians have endured for months since he shunted aside Parliament in what critics called a soft coup.

2021-12-14.abstract: Though both Omicron cases were quarantined upon arrival, a small outbreak in a building where one man was quarantining highlights how difficult it may be to control the new variant.

2021-12-14.title: Omicron is a dress rehearsal for the next pandemic.
2021-12-14.abstract: When scientists discovered the highly mutated Omicron variant of the coronavirus last month, it set off an eerily familiar chain of events.

2021-12-14.title: Belarus Opposition Leader Is Sentenced to 18 Years in Prison
2021-12-14.abstract: The activist Sergei Tikhanovsky planned to challenge the country’s authoritarian leader, Aleksandr G. Lukashenko, in a presidential election last year. He was arrested before the vote and his wife stepped in.

2021-12-14.title: Fuel Tanker Explodes in Haiti, Killing More Than 60
2021-12-14.abstract: Scores more were injured in the blast, the latest tragedy to hit a country that has been rocked by political violence, natural disasters, poverty and hunger.

2021-12-15.title: The Year in Pictures 2021
2021-12-15.abstract: While many people, fearing the virus, continued to stay close to home, photographers traveled the world, documenting the world’s turmoil and triumphs.

2021-12-15.title: Boat Carrying Indonesian Migrants Sinks Near Malaysia, Killing at Least 18
2021-12-15.abstract: The vessel capsized near a beach town in the South China Sea, leaving several others missing, officials said.

2021-12-15.title: Hong Kong Fire Traps Hundreds on Rooftop of Building
2021-12-15.abstract: The blaze at the World Trade Center in Causeway Bay injured at least 13 people, including one who was semiconscious, the police said.

2021-12-15.title: A Times correspondent returning from South Africa got caught up in the chaotic response to Omicron.
2021-12-15.abstract: The Omicron variant turned my trip home from South Africa into a nightmare episode of conflicting public health orders that often seemed to have little connection to science.

2021-12-15.title: The Nobel Peace Prize That Paved the Way for War
2021-12-15.abstract: This is the story behind how Ethiopia’s prime minister, Abiy Ahmed, won a Nobel Prize for making peace with his country’s longtime enemy — and then used the alliance to plan a war.

2021-12-16.title: Plane Crash in Dominican Republic Kills All 9 Aboard
2021-12-16.abstract: A prominent Puerto Rican music producer known as Flow La Movie was reported to be among the dead.

2021-12-16.title: 5 Schoolchildren Are Killed in Fall From Bouncy Castle in Australia
2021-12-16.abstract: A gust of wind swept up the inflatable on the last day of school, and the students fell more than 30 feet. Several others were critically injured.

2021-12-16.title: The Island Paradise Near the Front Line of Tensions Over Taiwan
2021-12-16.abstract: New missile batteries planned for the Japanese island of Ishigaki reflect a drastic shift in Japan’s views on China.

2021-12-16.title: NATO Signals Support for Ukraine in Face of Threat From Russia
2021-12-16.abstract: The military alliance stood by its promise to open a path to Ukrainian membership amid warnings from Western intelligence agencies that Moscow could soon begin a military incursion.

2021-12-16.title: France bans nonessential travel to Britain over the Omicron surge.
2021-12-16.abstract: The French prime minister said the restrictions were necessary after “the extremely rapid spread” of Omicron in Britain.

2021-12-17.title: 24 Dead in Suspected Arson at Office Building in Japan
2021-12-17.abstract: The fire consumed the fourth floor of the building in Osaka, which housed a medical office.

2021-12-17.title: Britain’s Conservatives Lose ‘Safe’ Seat, Dealing a Blow to Boris Johnson
2021-12-17.abstract: The governing party lost to the Liberal Democrats a district that it had represented for more than a century.

2021-12-17.title: The Giant Who Lives in the Melbourne Museum
2021-12-17.abstract: The world’s most complete triceratops skeleton ever found will go on display next year, displacing a beloved, if quirky, exhibit of taxidermy.

2021-12-17.title: ‘Time We Can’t Get Back’: Stolen at Birth, Chilean Adoptees Uncover Their Past
2021-12-17.abstract: Hundreds of Chileans adopted abroad have learned that they were trafficked. Investigators believe thousands of children may have been taken from their parents during Chile’s dictatorship.

2021-12-17.title: It spreads quickly, but just how severe is it? Omicron has scientists racing to gauge its threat.
2021-12-17.abstract: Even as scientists race to understand more about the Omicron variant and the threat it poses, one fact is abundantly clear: It spreads quickly everywhere it lands.

2021-12-18.title: The Novavax vaccine wins emergency approval from the W.H.O.
2021-12-18.abstract: The authorization could drastically increase the vaccine supply for low- and middle-income countries.

2021-12-18.title: Nets say Kyrie Irving can play at away games.
2021-12-18.abstract: The Nets will allow Kyrie Irving, whom they barred from the team until he received a Covid-19 vaccination, to rejoin the team part time, the Nets said in a statement on Friday, despite Irving’s still being unvaccinated and cases skyrocketing in New York City.

2021-12-18.title: How Hong Kong’s ‘Patriots Only’ Elections Bolster Beijing’s Grip
2021-12-18.abstract: China’s crackdown has driven most of the city’s popular pro-democracy candidates either into police custody or exile. Officials have urged the public to vote, but turnout Sunday hit a record low.

2021-12-18.title: Once a Symbol of U.S. Strength, an Afghan District Now Faces Dire Times
2021-12-18.abstract: At the war’s end, residents of Marja are growing increasingly desperate for any kind of help, a frustration that has turned to anger that the international community has seemingly abandoned them.

2021-12-18.title: Can a ‘Very Confident’ Carrie Lam Salvage Her Legacy in Hong Kong?
2021-12-18.abstract: Mrs. Lam is Hong Kong’s most unpopular leader ever, blamed for mass protests and a political crackdown. Yet she now appears reinvigorated, perhaps even ready for a second term.

2021-12-19.title: David Frost, Brexit Negotiator, Resigns in Another Blow to Boris Johnson
2021-12-19.abstract: The move puts new pressure on the prime minister, whose leadership has been called into question by a series of setbacks, including a new wave of Covid infections.

2021-12-19.title: Police Identify Suspect in Arson Fire That Killed 24 in Japan
2021-12-19.abstract: A 61-year-old man, hospitalized after the fire at an Osaka psychiatric clinic, is being investigated on suspicion of arson and murder, the police said.

2021-12-19.title: Some Southeast Asian tourism spots reopen, but arrivals are down from millions to dozens.
2021-12-19.abstract: Hopes for a revival of international tourism have fizzled amid restrictions, paperwork, a lack of flights and uncertainty around the Omicron variant.

2021-12-19.title: A Washington State senator died at 52 after a Covid infection.
2021-12-19.abstract: Doug Ericksen was a Republican who helped lead opposition to Washington State’s Covid-19 emergency orders and vaccine mandates.

2021-12-19.title: Biden will address the nation about Omicron on Tuesday as cases rise.
2021-12-19.abstract: The remarks are expected to detail how the United States will respond to the latest pandemic challenge.

2021-12-20.title: 1946: Europe Hit By Near-Zero Temperatures
2021-12-20.abstract: An unseasonable cold wave in many northern parts of the continent resulted in frozen waterways and deep snow.

2021-12-20.title: A Plan Forms in Mexico: Help Americans Get Abortions
2021-12-20.abstract: Mexican activists plan to provide women in Texas and other U.S. states with information, support — and abortion-inducing pills.

2021-12-20.title: Secret Charms and 7th Sons: ‘The Cure’ Is Alive and Well in Ireland
2021-12-20.abstract: Belief in the benefits of the cure, a type of folk medicine that interweaves home remedies with superstition, religion and a sprinkle of magic is still a way of life in pockets of Ireland.

2021-12-20.title: Israel bars its citizens from traveling to the U.S. or Canada.
2021-12-20.abstract: The countries were added to Israel’s rapidly growing “red list” as Prime Minister Naftali Bennett’s government tries to slow Omicron’s spread.

2021-12-20.title: Pope Says High Number of Domestic Violence Cases Is ‘Almost Satanic’
2021-12-20.abstract: Francis once again decried the scourge of domestic abuse, which has increased during the months of pandemic lockdown.

2021-12-21.title: Donald Trump said he got a booster shot and his supporters booed.
2021-12-21.abstract: At an event in Dallas, the former president revealed for the first time publicly that he had received the additional vaccine dose.

2021-12-21.title: How the Kremlin Is Militarizing Russian Society
2021-12-21.abstract: With a “youth army,” a cathedral honoring the military and state media promoting patriotism, the government is preparing Russians for the possibility of a fight.

2021-12-21.title: New Zealand and other Asia Pacific nations scramble to respond to Omicron.
2021-12-21.abstract: New Zealand is taking measures to limit the spread of Omicron, including delaying the reopening of its borders. It has detected 22 cases of Omicron in international arrivals.

2021-12-21.title: France finds over 180,000 fake Covid passes, and Italy makes arrests in suspected false inoculations.
2021-12-21.abstract: The developments suggest that resistance to Europe’s vaccination requirements remains significant.

2021-12-21.title: Cashing In Grandma’s Gold Coins, a Turkish Family Struggles to Get By
2021-12-21.abstract: As inflation soars and the value of Turkey’s currency plummets, one couple described how working families can’t make ends meet and have had to cut back on basic necessities as well as life’s smaller pleasures.

2021-12-22.title: Dozens Feared Dead After Landslide at Jade Mine in Myanmar
2021-12-22.abstract: An official said one person was killed after more than 70 were swept into a lake by mining waste in Hpakant, the center of the country’s secretive jade industry.

2021-12-22.title: A Tense Libya Delays Its Presidential Election
2021-12-22.abstract: The postponement risks further destabilizing the oil-rich North African country, which has been mired in divisions and violence in the decade since Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi was toppled and killed in a revolution.

2021-12-22.title: Using a City’s Excess Heat to Reduce Emissions
2021-12-22.abstract: A district in London has developed an innovative way to divert subway heat for buildings to lower carbon emissions. Other cities are getting creative, as well.

2021-12-22.title: U.K. System Strained as Health Care Workers Get Covid Themselves
2021-12-22.abstract: Hospitals, clinics and ambulance services say infections are winnowing their staffs, straining a system already taxed by nearly two years of dealing with the pandemic.

2021-12-22.title: Arrests, Beatings and Secret Prayers: Inside the Persecution of India’s Christians
2021-12-22.abstract: INDORE, India — The Christians were mid-hymn when the mob kicked in the door.

2021-12-23.title: ‘Koala Massacre’ Brings Hundreds of Animal Cruelty Charges
2021-12-23.abstract: A property owner and two companies in Australia were charged after a land-clearing operation left dozens of the marsupials dead, injured or starving.

2021-12-23.title: Of Mouth Molds and Michelin Stars: Chef Finds Fame After Epic Takedown
2021-12-23.abstract: Panned by a prominent blogger, the Italian restaurant Bros’ and its celebrity chef became a global target for critics of pretentious cuisine. There was a good side for him.

2021-12-23.title: Putin Mixes Positive Note With Threats, Keeping West on Edge
2021-12-23.abstract: At a traditional year-end news conference, President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia said a potential meeting with the U.S. was a positive sign, but he also demanded a quick response to his security concerns.

2021-12-23.title: Omicron is prompting rich nations to expand booster access. That may prolong the pandemic, the W.H.O. warns.
2021-12-23.abstract: As people in wealthy nations snap up booster shots amid the rapid spread of the Omicron variant, the World Health Organization’s leader warned that universal access to extra doses in highly inoculated countries could worsen global vaccine inequality and prolong the pandemic.

2021-12-23.title: China tells Winter Olympic spectators to clap, not cheer, for athletes, and Xi’an, a major city, locks down.
2021-12-23.abstract: As the Omicron variant spreads elsewhere, China is taking elaborate precautions to prevent the virus from reaching participants when the Games start in February.

2021-12-24.title: South Korea to Pardon Ex-President Park Geun-hye, Imprisoned for Corruption
2021-12-24.abstract: The government said it would release Ms. Park on Dec. 31 in the interest of national “reconciliation.”

2021-12-24.title: Fire on Crowded Bangladesh Ferry Leaves Dozens Dead
2021-12-24.abstract: Officials confirmed 35 deaths, but they said the toll was likely to rise as search and rescue continued.

2021-12-24.title: An Afghan Official Laments What Was Lost and Looks to What Lies Ahead
2021-12-24.abstract: Ahmad Nader Nadery spent decades working toward a thriving, free Afghanistan. Watching the Taliban undo much of his work, he wonders how to move forward.

2021-12-24.title: Europeans Ponder Living With, Not Defeating, Covid
2021-12-24.abstract: On the eve of the biggest holidays in Europe, exhaustion with the pandemic is rising. So is resignation that the virus is endemic.

2021-12-24.title: Queen Elizabeth II is having a quiet Christmas, her first since Prince Philip’s death.
2021-12-24.abstract: The 95-year-old monarch is spending the holiday at Windsor Castle instead of Sandringham, her English country estate.

2021-12-25.title: Many African Countries Toughen Covid Restrictions as Fourth Wave Spreads
2021-12-25.abstract: Infections have more than doubled or tripled in many countries, prompting the authorities to reimpose curfews and quarantines and introduce vaccine mandates as the holiday season gets underway.

2021-12-25.title: A New Israeli Wonderland, Where You Can Almost Forget Where You Are
2021-12-25.abstract: An alien spaceship theme park, an Italian Renaissance-style mall, a canal with gondolas. “Nothing in Israel compares with it,” the developer says. There’s just one catch.

2021-12-25.title: Another Covid Christmas …
2021-12-25.abstract: December went by like an Omicron advent calendar, each passing day revealing how the virus’s trajectory would change hoped-for holiday plans.

2021-12-25.title: As Covid Disrupts a 2nd Christmas, Pope Prays for Healing and Peace
2021-12-25.abstract: In his Christmas message, Pope Francis called on world leaders to resolve persisting conflicts and prayed for people across the globe who are affected by the coronavirus.

2021-12-25.title: In China, Bragging About Your Wealth Can Get You Censored
2021-12-25.abstract: Online posts by users showing off their receipts, over-ordering food or scattering money have been deemed vulgar. Regulators say such content leads young people astray.

2021-12-26.title: Desmond Tutu, Whose Voice Helped Slay Apartheid, Dies at 90
2021-12-26.abstract: The archbishop, a powerful force for nonviolence in South Africa’s anti-apartheid movement, was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1984.

2021-12-26.title: Training Civilians, Ukraine Nurtures a Resistance in Waiting
2021-12-26.abstract: Eastern European nations have drawn a lesson from America’s wars of the last decades: Insurgency works. Ukraine’s training of volunteers has become a factor in the standoff with Russia.

2021-12-26.title: Myanmar’s Army Is Accused of Massacring Dozens of Civilians
2021-12-26.abstract: At least 35 people were killed and their bodies burned, according to an international aid group and opponents of the military regime.

2021-12-26.title: ‘A True South African Giant’: Tributes for Desmond Tutu, a Force for Harmony
2021-12-26.abstract: From prominent South African lawmakers to religious figures, many on social media honored Archbishop Tutu as a patriot of peaceful principles who dedicated his life to combating injustice everywhere.

2021-12-26.title: After Chaotic Evacuation, Afghans in the Netherlands Struggle to Find Stability
2021-12-26.abstract: Many Afghans evacuated to the Netherlands have been living in tents for months, as they prepare to resettle in a Europe that is wrangling with a heated debate about immigration.

2021-12-27.title: ‘Help Us’: After Typhoon Rai, Miles of Destruction and the Smell of Death
2021-12-27.abstract: Philippine officials warned that residents were going hungry. Many were still in evacuation centers. On a highway, survivors scrawled an appeal for aid.

2021-12-27.title: As Other Arab States Falter, Saudi Arabia Seeks to Become a Cultural Hub
2021-12-27.abstract: While conflicts and crises have battered Arab cultural capitals, Saudi Arabia is hosting film festivals and bankrolling new movies.

2021-12-27.title: A lab in Australia mistakenly tells nearly 900 people they tested negative.
2021-12-27.abstract: Human error was to blame for the mix-up amid a high demand for Covid testing, a clinic in Sydney said.

2021-12-27.title: Somalia’s President Suspends Prime Minister Over Corruption Allegations
2021-12-27.abstract: The premier, Mohamed Hussein Roble, defied the order to step down as tensions continued over long-delayed elections.

2021-12-27.title: South Korea approves Pfizer’s coronavirus pill for emergency use.
2021-12-27.abstract: It is the first pill approved in the nation to treat symptoms of the coronavirus.

2021-12-28.title: Germany Wants Its Russian Pipeline. German Allies Aren’t Sure It’s a Good Idea.
2021-12-28.abstract: The Nord Stream 2 Russia-to-Germany pipeline could offer plentiful natural gas. But tensions with Vladimir Putin are keeping it out of reach.

2021-12-28.title: ‘Guide Our Shot’: An Italian Town Roots for Its Patron Saint of Basketball
2021-12-28.abstract: Porretta Terme, in central Italy, is passionate about basketball, and is pressing the Vatican to officially recognize its local saint as Italy’s patron of the sport.

2021-12-28.title: Covid now accounts for a larger share of deaths among white and young Americans.
2021-12-28.abstract: Covid-19 deaths have declined since vaccines became widely available in April, yet nearly a quarter million people in the United States have passed away from the virus in the last eight months.

2021-12-28.title: Russian Court Orders Prominent Human Rights Group to Shut
2021-12-28.abstract: The Supreme Court ruled that Memorial International, which chronicled political repression in Russia, must be liquidated.

2021-12-28.title: An Australian teenager who partied while positive is charged with ignoring health orders.
2021-12-28.abstract: A 19-year-old man who kept partying at a nightclub in South Australia despite being notified that he had tested positive for the coronavirus was charged with failing to comply with public health orders, the police said on Tuesday.

2021-12-29.title: In Quebec, some health care workers who test positive will continue working.
2021-12-29.abstract: All essential workers in the province, including those who work in medicine, will be subject to the new rules.

2021-12-29.title: Hong Kong Police Raid Office of Pro-Democracy News Site and Arrest 7
2021-12-29.abstract: Those arrested, including current and former senior staff members of Stand News, were accused of conspiring to publish seditious material. The news site announced it would shut down immediately.

2021-12-29.title: The U.S. record for daily cases is broken as an Omicron ‘tidal wave’ grows.
2021-12-29.abstract: As a third year of the pandemic loomed, the seven-day average of U.S. cases topped 267,000 on Tuesday.

2021-12-29.title: Two Georgia Republicans have racked up $100,000 in fines for defying a mask mandate in Congress.
2021-12-29.abstract: During a recent marathon session in the House, two Republican lawmakers from Georgia sat in full view of television cameras. Neither was wearing a mask.

2021-12-29.title: As the Kremlin Revises History, a Human Rights Champion Becomes a Casualty
2021-12-29.abstract: The shuttering of Memorial, the country’s most prominent human rights organization, has saddened Russians who were personally touched by its work shining a light on the injustices of the Soviet past.

2021-12-30.title: World Junior Championship, a showcase for hockey’s top prospects, is canceled.
2021-12-30.abstract: The cancellation was an ominous sign for sports leagues, including the N.H.L. and the N.B.A., which have been struggling to maintain schedules.

2021-12-30.title: The U.S. breaks its single-day case record, nearly doubling the highest numbers from last winter.
2021-12-30.abstract: The new daily case total topped 488,000 on Wednesday, according to a New York Times database.

2021-12-30.title: In 2021, We Were There: The Year’s 14 Most Popular Dispatches
2021-12-30.abstract: Not even a pandemic, wars or autocrats could prevent our correspondents from seeking out secrets and surprises to share with readers. They sent dispatches from 40 countries on six continents.

2021-12-30.title: A World Divided by Covid and Other Ills United to Explore Space
2021-12-30.abstract: “I’ve always seen space as an area where we cooperate, through all the trying times,” said a professor who oversaw mission control for the global effort to launch a $10 billion telescope into space.

2021-12-30.title: Can a Tiny Territory in the South Pacific Power Tesla’s Ambitions?
2021-12-30.abstract: Nickel is vital to electric car batteries, but extracting it is dirty and destructive. A plant with a turbulent history in New Caledonia is about to become an experiment in doing it better.

2021-12-31.title: Suspect in Deadly Arson Fire in Osaka, Japan, Dies in Hospital
2021-12-31.abstract: The suspect, a 61-year-old man, had been hospitalized for smoke inhalation since the Dec. 17 fire at a psychiatric clinic, which killed 25 other people.

2021-12-31.title: Omicron Strains France’s Social Contract on Covid
2021-12-31.abstract: President Emmanuel Macron won people over by promising normalcy if they followed Covid advice. Now Omicron has raised questions about whether he, or any leader, can deliver on that.

2021-12-31.title: Is That a Burning Bush? Is This Mt. Sinai? Solstice Bolsters a Claim
2021-12-31.abstract: On the year’s shortest day, hundreds of Israelis ventured deep into the desert to witness a strange natural phenomenon atop an ancient pilgrimage site that some argue is where God spoke to Moses.

2021-12-31.title: Paris reintroduces an outdoor mask mandate.
2021-12-31.abstract: With more than 2,000 coronavirus cases per 100,000 people, the capital has become the center of France’s spreading Omicron outbreak.

2021-12-31.title: In Remembering Tutu, South Africa Reckons With Lasting Challenges
2021-12-31.abstract: Archbishop Desmond Tutu’s death has reignited a public conversation about the state of the country’s reconciliation process and its democracy.
